decisions.before: The PRD describes the core problem: "Most early-stage UK founders are networking with people who can't buy from them — peers at similar stages with no budget and no relevant customer base." decisions.title: Answered open question on phases_and_sizing.timeline
decisions.description: User answer merged into field: MVP Q3 2026, MMP Q1 2027.
decisions.rationale: User answer merged into field: MVP Q3 2026, MMP Q1 2027.
decisions.before: The PRD provides relative phase durations but no calendar dates. It states the MVP is a learning instrument and the MMP follows after success criteria are met, but exact timelines are absent. Therefore the field is marked partial.
decisions.after: The PRD provides relative phase durations but no calendar dates. However, the stakeholder specifies that the MVP is planned for Q3 2026 and the MMP for Q1 2027. It states the MVP is a learning instrument and the MMP follows after success criteria are met, but exact timelines are now provided. Therefore the field is marked partial.

decisions.title: Answered open question on technical_approach.technical_approach
decisions.description: User answer merged into field: Store all data in UK‑based Supabase region with Vercel EU edge.
decisions.rationale: User answer merged into field: Store all data in UK‑based Supabase region with Vercel EU edge.
decisions.before: The technical stack described in the PRD includes: "Custom web application (Next.js)"; "Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Realtime + Storage)"; "Stripe (subscriptions, webhooks, Customer Portal)"; and "Vercel (hosting)". These technologies provide the language/framework (React/Next.js) and persistence layer (PostgreSQL via Supabase).
decisions.after: The technical stack described in the PRD includes: "Custom web application (Next.js)"; "Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Realtime + Storage)"; "Stripe (subscriptions, webhooks, Customer Portal)"; and "Vercel (hosting)". These technologies provide the language/framework (React/Next.js) and persistence layer (PostgreSQL via Supabase). Additionally, to enforce data residency constraints for UK founders, all data will be stored in a UK‑based Supabase region, and the application will be delivered throug...

decisions.title: Answered open question on phases_and_sizing.dependency_order
decisions.description: User answer merged into field: Supabase → Stripe → Resend
decisions.rationale: User answer merged into field: Supabase → Stripe → Resend
decisions.before: The PRD lists dependencies but does not define a precise order. Implicitly, the MVP (manual matching, Slack community) precedes the MMP (custom app). Additionally, Stripe billing requires user accounts stored in Supabase, suggesting Supabase must be configured before Stripe integration. The lack of an explicit chain makes this field partial.
decisions.after: The PRD lists dependencies but does not define a precise order. Implicitly, the MVP (manual matching, Slack community) precedes the MMP (custom app). Additionally, Stripe billing requires user accounts stored in Supabase, suggesting Supabase must be configured before Stripe integration. The stakeholder clarifies the exact sequence of external integrations that must be completed before MMP can go live: Supabase → Stripe → Resend. This explicit chain confirms the implied ordering and completes the...

decisions.title: Answered open question on phases_and_sizing.effort_estimates
decisions.description: User answer merged into field: MVP 2 person‑weeks, MMP 8 person‑weeks.
decisions.rationale: User answer merged into field: MVP 2 person‑weeks, MMP 8 person‑weeks.
decisions.before: The PRD does not specify exact person‑weeks or cost for building the MVP or MMP. It describes the MVP stack (Framer/ Carrd, Typeform, Airtable) and the MMP stack (Next.js, Supabase, Stripe) but provides no effort sizing. Consequently the field is marked partial.
decisions.after: The PRD does not specify exact person‑weeks or cost for building the MVP or MMP. It describes the MVP stack (Framer/ Carrd, Typeform, Airtable) and the MMP stack (Next.js, Supabase, Stripe). However, the stakeholder estimates that the MVP will require approximately 2 person‑weeks and the MMP about 8 person‑weeks. Consequently the field is marked partial.
